explicit_text: I grew up in Brooklyn Park, Minnesota, where life was pretty calm. I lived in an apartment with just my mom and uncle, and although my neighbors were noisy and often had parties, I enjoyed spending summers going to the pool and parks nearby. Brooklyn Park was my home during my childhood, and even though it was quiet, I always felt connected to the community around me.

explicit_text: In the summer of 2011, I traveled to the Dominican Republic for the first time to meet my grandparents, whom I hadn't seen since I was three years old. I went with my aunt, cousins, and brother, and later my dad joined us because my grandfather had undergone surgery and might not have much time left. Visiting the Dominican Republic was also special because I got to meet my older sister for the first time, which was something I had always wanted since childhood.

explicit_text: I have lived most of my life in the United States, specifically in Minnesota, where my family and I have settled. My grandparents often travel to the U.S., but they usually visit New Jersey rather than Minnesota, where my aunt and other relatives live. The U.S. has been my home, but my roots and family connections stretch between here and places like the Dominican Republic and New Jersey.
